rule: Avoid self-directed herbs and formulations during pregnancy. Use only with qualified medical and Ayurvedic supervision.
action: Suppress suggestions and advise qualified review.

rule: Breastfeeding requires individualized safety review because constituents may pass to the infant.
action: Show conservative options only with qualified review.

rule: Children should not receive self-directed herbal recommendations from this tool.
action: Suppress suggestions and advise qualified review.

rule: Older adults may have higher interaction and adverse event risk, especially with multiple medicines.
Some herbs may affect thyroid status or medicine timing.
action: Show conservative options and flag clinician review for medicine timing and monitoring.

rule: Herbs may interact with anticoagulant or antiplatelet medicines.
action: Show conservative options and flag clinician review before use.

rule: Glucose-lowering medicines can interact with herbs that affect blood sugar.
action: Show conservative options and flag clinician review before use.

rule: Blood pressure conditions and medicines require review before herbal use.
Liver disease raises safety concerns for many herbs and formulations.
action: Suppress suggestions and advise clinician review.

rule: Kidney disease changes safety considerations for herbs, minerals, and electrolytes.
action: Suppress suggestions and advise clinician review.

rule: Immune-modulating herbs require clinician review in autoimmune disease or immunosuppressant use.
Sedating herbs may add to drowsiness or impairment.
action: Avoid sedating suggestions without qualified review.

rule: Known allergy to a plant, herb, or related family is a reason to avoid that substance.
action: Show avoid/caution list and advise qualified review.
